assertion.title: sqrtdiv(a,b)=sqrt(a/b) compound operator reduces total nodes 288→283
assertion.statement: Adding a binary compound operator sqrtdiv(a,b)=sqrt(a/b) saves 5 nodes across 5 equations by absorbing the unary sqrt wrapping a div into a single binary operator, reducing total nodes from 288 to 283 while maintaining 50/50 exact matches.

details.hypothesis: Binary compound operator sqrtdiv(a,b)=sqrt(a/b) can replace sqrt(div(a,b)) patterns, each saving 1 node by absorbing the unary sqrt into the binary operator.
changes: Added sqrtdiv binary operator: sqrtdiv(a,b) = sqrt(a/b) with safe division
Added simplification rule: sqrt(div(X, Y)) → sqrtdiv(X, Y)
Updated permutation templates: n=2 (sqrtdiv for pendulum period), n=3 (sqrtdiv for escape/orbital/rms velocity), n=4 (sqrtdiv for pendulum angle)
Updated random-variable physics templates for consistency
details.key_insight: The sqrt(a/b) pattern appears frequently in physics for quantities involving ratios under square roots: pendulum period sqrt(l/g), escape velocity sqrt(2GM/r), RMS speed sqrt(k_BT/m). Each wraps sqrt() around div(), costing 1 extra node. A dedicated binary operator absorbs both operations, saving 1 node per occurrence.
